title: Untrusted Assets, Save Parsing, Paths, Privacy, and Local Security · Language Clinic: Threat Model
summary: A mod folder escapes its root, decompression consumes memory, pickle loads scores, save files are unlimited, crash reports include home paths, and telemetry starts without consent. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the threat model increment in bounded local-data and privacy policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chan…
objectives: Identify protected player data and availability, untrusted files and paths, package supply chain, local attackers, and realistic consequences.
Inventory origin, permission, transformation, attribution, package inclusion, fallback, and removal for every asset.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Threat Model · Theory Threat Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 1645 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Threat Model contract for the supplied data.

Apply that move specifically to Threat Model while producing bounded local-data and privacy policy.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 1645 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify protected player data and availability, untrusted files and paths, package supply chain, local attackers, and realistic consequences. A defensible solution applies Threat Model, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded local-data and privacy policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-threat-model — Identify protected player data and availability, untrusted files and paths, package supply chain, local attackers, and realistic consequences.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-security-save-assets-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-security-save-assets-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_security_save_assets_3(untrusted_paths: int = 6, rejected_paths: int = 6, save_size: int = 4096, save_limit: int = 65536, remote_content: bool = False, telemetry_opt_in: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if untrusted_paths < 0 or rejected_paths < 0:
        return False, rejected_paths, "security-domain"
    if rejected_paths != untrusted_paths:
        return False, rejected_paths, "path-admission"
    if save_size < 0 or save_size > save_limit:
        return False, save_size, "save-budget"
    if remote_content or not telemetry_opt_in:
        return False, save_size, "external-data"
    assert save_size <= save_limit
    return True, rejected_paths, "bounded-local-data"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Threat Model evidence · Theory Threat Model
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-threat-model-1ulob6" that uses case 4038 to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Threat Model evidence · Theory Threat Model.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Threat Model evidence · Theory Threat Model, keep Threat Model connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-threat-model-1ulob6
# Competency: asteroids-threat-model.
# Case 4038: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_threat_model_1ulob6(untrusted_paths: int = 6, rejected_paths: int = 6, save_size: int = 4096, save_limit: int = 65536, remote_content: bool = False, telemetry_opt_in: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1ulob6 = "1ulob6-6bolu1-4038"
    assert evidence_variant_1ulob6
    evidence_axes_1ulob6 = (
        "player1ulob6",
        "seed4038",
        "input6bolu1",
        "frame1ulob64038",
        "state40381ulob6",
        "playtest6bolu14038",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1ulob6)) == 6
    if untrusted_paths < 0 or rejected_paths < 0:
        return False, rejected_paths, "security-domain"
    if rejected_paths != untrusted_paths:
        return False, rejected_paths, "path-admission"
    if save_size < 0 or save_size > save_limit:
        return False, save_size, "save-budget"
    if remote_content or not telemetry_opt_in:
        return False, save_size, "external-data"
    assert save_size <= save_limit
    return True, rejected_paths, "bounded-local-data"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Identify protected player data and availability, untrusted files and paths, package supply chain, local attackers, and realistic consequences.
